states.name: Alabama
facts: Alabama was a key battleground during the Civil Rights Movement
The U.S. Space & Rocket Center in Huntsville is the world's largest space museum
Alabama is the only state with all the natural resources needed to make iron and steel
The first electric trolley system in the US was built in Montgomery
Alabama has more species of freshwater fish than any other state

states.name: Alaska
facts: Alaska is the largest state in the US — bigger than Texas, California, and Montana combined
Alaska has more coastline than all other US states combined
Denali in Alaska is the highest peak in North America at 20,310 feet
Alaska has more active glaciers than the rest of the inhabited world combined
In some parts of Alaska, the sun doesn't set for over 70 consecutive days in summer

states.name: Arizona
facts: The Grand Canyon is one of the Seven Natural Wonders of the World
Arizona is the only state in the contiguous US that doesn't observe Daylight Saving Time
The saguaro cactus, found only in Arizona and Mexico, can live for over 150 years
Arizona is the copper-producing capital of the US
Meteor Crater in Arizona is the best-preserved meteorite impact site on Earth

states.name: Arkansas
facts: The only diamond-producing mine in North America open to the public is in Arkansas
Walmart, the world's largest retailer, was founded in Rogers, Arkansas
Arkansas has 52 state parks — more per square mile than any other state
Hot Springs, Arkansas became the first federally protected land area in US history in 1832
Arkansas is the leading producer of rice in the United States

states.name: California
facts: Home to Silicon Valley, the tech capital of the world
Has the largest economy of any US state
Death Valley is the hottest place in North America
Home to the world's tallest trees — the Coast Redwoods
California produces over 80% of the world's almonds

states.name: Colorado
facts: Colorado has the highest mean elevation of any US state
Colorado was the first state to legalize recreational marijuana use
Denver is called the 'Mile High City' because it sits exactly one mile above sea level
Colorado is home to the headwaters of the Colorado, Rio Grande, and Arkansas rivers
Colorado has more fourteeners (peaks over 14,000 ft) than any other state — 58 in total

states.name: Connecticut
facts: Connecticut's Fundamental Orders of 1639 is considered the world's first written constitution
Yale University, one of the world's top universities, is in New Haven, Connecticut
Connecticut is the third smallest state but one of the wealthiest per capita
The first speed limit law in the US was enacted in Connecticut in 1901
The hamburger is said to have been invented in New Haven, Connecticut

states.name: Delaware
facts: Delaware was the first state to ratify the US Constitution on December 7, 1787
Delaware is the second smallest state in the US
More than half of all Fortune 500 companies are incorporated in Delaware
Delaware is the only state without a national park
The DuPont company, a global chemical giant, was founded in Wilmington, Delaware

states.name: Florida
facts: Florida has more theme parks than any other state, including Walt Disney World
Florida is the flattest state in the US
The Everglades is the largest subtropical wilderness in the US
Florida produces over 70% of all oranges grown in the US
Florida has the longest coastline of the contiguous 48 states

states.name: Georgia
facts: Coca-Cola was invented in Atlanta, Georgia in 1886
Georgia is the largest state east of the Mississippi River
Georgia produces more peanuts than any other state
Atlanta is home to the world's busiest airport — Hartsfield-Jackson
Georgia is the birthplace of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.

states.name: Hawaii
facts: Hawaii is the only US state made entirely of islands
Hawaii is the most isolated population center on Earth
The Hawaiian alphabet has only 13 letters
Hawaii is the only US state that grows coffee commercially
Mauna Kea in Hawaii is the world's tallest mountain when measured from the ocean floor

states.name: Idaho
facts: Idaho produces about one-third of all potatoes grown in the United States
Idaho has more than 3,100 miles of whitewater rivers — more than any other state
Craters of the Moon National Monument in Idaho looks like the surface of the moon
Idaho has the deepest river gorge in North America — Hells Canyon
The state of Idaho gets its name from a Shoshone phrase meaning 'the sun comes from the mountains'

states.name: Illinois
facts: The first McDonald's franchise was opened in Des Plaines, Illinois in 1955
Chicago, the third largest US city, is in Illinois and sits on Lake Michigan
Abraham Lincoln, the 16th president, spent most of his life in Illinois
The first skyscraper in the world was built in Chicago in 1885
Illinois is the leading producer of soybeans in the United States

states.name: Indiana
facts: The Indianapolis 500 is the world's largest single-day sporting event
Indiana is the popcorn capital of the world
More astronauts have come from Indiana than from any other state
The first professional baseball game was played in Fort Wayne, Indiana in 1871
Indiana's steel industry produces more steel than any other state

states.name: Iowa
facts: Iowa grows more corn than any other state, earning it the nickname 'Corn State'
Iowa has the highest literacy rate in the US
Iowa was the first state to allow women to practice law, in 1869
Iowa is the only state whose east and west borders are both formed by rivers
The Iowa caucuses are historically the first major electoral event in presidential elections

states.name: Kansas
facts: Kansas is the geographic center of the contiguous 48 states
Kansas produces more wheat than any other state and is called 'The Breadbasket of America'
The world's largest collection of helicopters is at the Kansas Aviation Museum
Dodge City, Kansas was the wildest town in the Wild West during the 1870s
Kansas is one of the top states for wind energy production

states.name: Kentucky
facts: Kentucky is home to Mammoth Cave, the world's longest known cave system
Over 95% of the world's bourbon whiskey is produced in Kentucky
The Kentucky Derby, held every May, is the oldest continuously held major sporting event in the US
Kentucky Fried Chicken (KFC) was founded in Corbin, Kentucky
Fort Knox, which holds most of the US gold reserves, is in Kentucky

states.name: Louisiana
facts: Louisiana is the only state that still uses the Napoleonic Code in its legal system
New Orleans is the jazz music capital of the world
Mardi Gras has been celebrated in New Orleans since 1699
Louisiana is the most below-sea-level state — New Orleans is up to 7 feet below sea level
Louisiana has more plant and animal species per square mile than most other states

states.name: Maine
facts: Maine is the easternmost state in the US
Maine produces 99% of all wild blueberries in the United States
Maine has more shoreline than California despite being much smaller
Maine is the only US state that borders just one other state
Maine produces over 90% of the US lobster supply

states.name: Maryland
facts: Maryland is the birthplace of the US Naval Academy, located in Annapolis
The National Anthem, The Star-Spangled Banner, was written by Francis Scott Key during the Battle of Fort McHenry in Maryland
Maryland is known as 'America in Miniature' because it has diverse landscapes
Maryland produces more blue crabs than any other state
The Chesapeake Bay, the largest estuary in the US, borders Maryland

states.name: Massachusetts
facts: Massachusetts is home to Harvard, the oldest university in the US, founded in 1636
Basketball and volleyball were both invented in Massachusetts
The first subway system in the US opened in Boston in 1897
The Pilgrims landed at Plymouth Rock, Massachusetts in 1620
Massachusetts was the first state to legalize same-sex marriage in the US

states.name: Michigan
facts: Michigan is the only state that consists of two separate peninsulas
Detroit is the auto capital of the world — home to Ford, GM, and Chrysler
Michigan has the longest freshwater shoreline of any state
Kellogg's and Post cereals were both founded in Battle Creek, Michigan
Michigan has more lighthouses than any other state

states.name: Minnesota
facts: Minnesota actually has over 11,000 lakes — more than any other state except Alaska
Mall of America in Bloomington is the largest shopping mall in the US
Minnesota is the birthplace of both Target and Best Buy
The Mississippi River begins as a tiny stream in Lake Itasca, Minnesota
Post-it Notes were invented by 3M, a company based in Minnesota

states.name: Mississippi
facts: Mississippi is named after the Mississippi River, which forms its western border
The teddy bear was named after President Theodore Roosevelt following a bear-hunting trip in Mississippi
Mississippi was the last state to ratify the 13th Amendment abolishing slavery — in 1995
The blues music genre originated in the Mississippi Delta
Mississippi has the most churches per capita of any US state

states.name: Missouri
facts: The Gateway Arch in St. Louis is the tallest man-made monument in the US
Missouri is the starting point of both the Lewis and Clark Expedition and the Pony Express
Kansas City, Missouri has more barbecue restaurants per capita than any other US city
Missouri is the birthplace of Mark Twain and Harry S. Truman
The ice cream cone was invented at the 1904 St. Louis World's Fair

states.name: Montana
facts: Montana is the fourth largest state but has one of the smallest populations
Montana has three times more cattle than people
Glacier National Park in Montana straddles the US-Canada border
Montana has the largest grizzly bear population outside of Alaska
Montana has only one area code for the entire state

states.name: Nebraska
facts: Nebraska is the only state with a unicameral (single-chamber) legislature
Nebraska has more miles of river than any other state
Warren Buffett, one of the world's wealthiest people, was born and lives in Omaha, Nebraska
Nebraska is the top state for Runza (a type of meat pastry) consumption in the US
Chimney Rock in Nebraska was the most famous landmark on the Oregon Trail

states.name: Nevada
facts: Las Vegas, Nevada is the entertainment capital of the world
Nevada is the driest state in the US — most of it is desert
Nevada produces more gold than any other state
Nevada was the first state to allow casino gambling, in 1931
Area 51, the secretive US Air Force base, is in Nevada

states.name: New Hampshire
facts: New Hampshire is the first state to hold presidential primaries every four years
Mount Washington in New Hampshire once recorded the world's highest surface wind speed — 231 mph
New Hampshire has no state income tax and no state sales tax
New Hampshire's state motto is 'Live Free or Die', the most striking motto of any US state
New Hampshire was one of the original 13 colonies

states.name: New Jersey
facts: New Jersey is the most densely populated state in the US
Thomas Edison invented the light bulb at his laboratory in Menlo Park, New Jersey
New Jersey has more diners than any other state in the world
Bruce Springsteen, Frank Sinatra, and Bon Jovi all come from New Jersey
New Jersey has the most shopping malls per square mile of any US state

states.name: New Mexico
facts: New Mexico has more PhDs per capita than any other state
The first atomic bomb was tested in New Mexico at the Trinity Site in 1945
Santa Fe is the oldest state capital in the US, founded around 1610
New Mexico's Carlsbad Caverns holds the largest natural limestone cave in North America
New Mexico has the largest number of hot-air balloon festivals in the US

states.name: New York
facts: New York City is home to more than 800 languages, making it the most linguistically diverse city in the world
The Statue of Liberty was a gift from France and stands on Liberty Island in New York Harbor
New York City's subway system is the largest in the Western Hemisphere
New York Stock Exchange on Wall Street is the world's largest stock exchange by market cap
New York City's Central Park is larger than the country of Monaco

states.name: North Carolina
facts: The Wright Brothers made the first successful powered airplane flight at Kitty Hawk, North Carolina
North Carolina is the nation's top producer of sweet potatoes
North Carolina is home to the highest peak east of the Mississippi — Mount Mitchell
Pepsi-Cola was invented in New Bern, North Carolina in 1898
The Great Smoky Mountains, the most visited national park in the US, is partly in North Carolina

states.name: North Dakota
facts: North Dakota is the least visited state in the US
North Dakota leads the US in production of sunflowers, canola, and flaxseed
North Dakota sits atop one of the world's largest oil reserves
The geographic center of North America is in Rugby, North Dakota
North Dakota has more wildlife refuges than any other state in the contiguous US

states.name: Ohio
facts: Ohio has produced more US presidents than any other state — 8 in total
Neil Armstrong, the first man to walk on the moon, was from Wapakoneta, Ohio
The first professional baseball team, the Cincinnati Red Stockings, was formed in Ohio in 1869
Ohio is the birthplace of aviation — Wilbur and Orville Wright were from Dayton
Rock and Roll Hall of Fame is in Cleveland, Ohio
landmarks: Rock and Roll Hall of Fame

states.name: Oklahoma
facts: Oklahoma has more man-made lakes than any other state
Oklahoma experiences more tornadoes per square mile than any other state
Oklahoma City is one of the few state capitals to have oil wells on its capitol grounds
The parking meter was invented in Oklahoma City in 1935
Oklahoma has 39 tribal nations — more than any other state

states.name: Oregon
facts: Crater Lake in Oregon is the deepest lake in the US, formed in an ancient volcano
Oregon is the only state with a different design on each side of its flag
Portland, Oregon has the most microbreweries per capita of any city in the world
Oregon produces more Christmas trees than any other state
The Oregon Trail, used by about 500,000 settlers, ended in Oregon's Willamette Valley

states.name: Pennsylvania
facts: The Declaration of Independence and the US Constitution were both signed in Philadelphia
Pennsylvania has the largest rural population of any state
Punxsutawney Phil, the famous groundhog who predicts the weather, is from Pennsylvania
The first commercial oil well in the US was drilled in Titusville, Pennsylvania in 1859
Pennsylvania is the top producer of mushrooms in the US

states.name: Rhode Island
facts: Rhode Island is the smallest state in the US — it's 37 miles wide and 48 miles long
Rhode Island has the longest official state name: 'State of Rhode Island and Providence Plantations'
The first circus in the US was held in Newport, Rhode Island in 1774
Rhode Island was the last of the original 13 colonies to ratify the Constitution
The first American-built steam engine was made in Pawtucket, Rhode Island

states.name: South Carolina
facts: The first battle of the Civil War took place at Fort Sumter in Charleston, South Carolina
South Carolina is the top peach-producing state on the East Coast
The Angel Oak tree on Johns Island is estimated to be 400-500 years old
South Carolina produces more peaches than Georgia
Myrtle Beach in South Carolina has over 100 golf courses — one of the top golf destinations in the US

states.name: South Dakota
facts: Mount Rushmore, carved into the Black Hills, features four US presidents
South Dakota is home to Wind Cave, one of the world's longest cave systems
The Crazy Horse Memorial in South Dakota will be larger than Mount Rushmore when complete
Wall Drug in South Dakota is one of the most famous roadside attractions in the US
Badlands National Park in South Dakota has the world's richest fossil beds

states.name: Tennessee
facts: Nashville, Tennessee is the country music capital of the world
Tennessee borders more states than any other — eight in total
Memphis, Tennessee is where Elvis Presley lived — Graceland is there
The Great Smoky Mountains, the most visited US national park, is in Tennessee
Tennessee was one of the last states to secede during the Civil War and the first to be readmitted
landmarks: Country Music Hall of Fame Nashville

states.name: Texas
facts: Texas is the second largest state in the US by both area and population
Texas was an independent nation — the Republic of Texas — for 10 years before joining the US
Texas produces more oil than any other US state
Texas is the only state that was once an independent country and has the right to fly its flag at the same height as the US flag
The King Ranch in Texas is larger than the state of Rhode Island

states.name: Utah
facts: Utah has more national parks than almost any other state — Zion, Bryce Canyon, Arches, Capitol Reef, and Canyonlands
The Great Salt Lake is the largest saltwater lake in the Western Hemisphere
Utah has the highest literacy rate in the US
The transcontinental railroad was completed at Promontory Summit, Utah in 1869
Utah hosted the 2002 Winter Olympics in Salt Lake City

states.name: Vermont
facts: Vermont produces more maple syrup per person than any other US state
Montpelier is the smallest US state capital city by population
Vermont was the first state admitted to the Union after the original 13 colonies
Vermont is the second least populated state in the US
Ben & Jerry's Ice Cream was founded in Burlington, Vermont

states.name: Virginia
facts: Virginia is called the 'Mother of Presidents' — 8 US presidents were born there
The Pentagon, the world's largest office building, is in Arlington, Virginia
Virginia Beach, Virginia is the most populous city in the state
More Civil War battles were fought in Virginia than in any other state
Jamestown, Virginia was the first permanent English settlement in North America, founded in 1607

states.name: Washington
facts: Washington is the only state named after a US president
Microsoft, Amazon, and Starbucks all started in Washington State
Mount Rainier in Washington is the most glaciated peak in the contiguous US
Washington produces more apples than any other state
Seattle's Pike Place Market is one of the oldest continuously operating farmers markets in the US

states.name: West Virginia
facts: West Virginia is the only state that was formed by seceding from another state (Virginia) during the Civil War
West Virginia has the highest mean elevation east of the Mississippi River
The New River in West Virginia is one of the oldest rivers in the world despite its name
West Virginia is the leading US producer of coal
More than 75% of West Virginia is covered by forest

states.name: Wisconsin
facts: Wisconsin produces more cheese than any other state — about 3 billion pounds per year
Wisconsin is home to the world's largest music festival — Summerfest in Milwaukee
The Republican Party was founded in Ripon, Wisconsin in 1854
Wisconsin has more dairy cows per square mile than any other state
The typewriter was invented in Milwaukee, Wisconsin in 1868

states.name: Wyoming
facts: Wyoming is the least populated state in the US
Wyoming was the first state to give women the right to vote, in 1869
Yellowstone, the world's first national park, is mostly in Wyoming
Wyoming has more geysers than any other place on Earth
Wyoming is the eighth largest state but has fewer people than many cities
